title: Lesson 351
transcript: So we've been talking about controls that need to be applied in database environments. And specifically, we talked about concurrency controls and lock controls. In fact, in the last video, You saw the acid test and what that represents in addressing, for example, law controls. A very short video here that talks about what we call metadata, because this word is seen not only in the exam outline, but obviously in the exam as well. through questions, but they may ask you, for example, what is the definition of metadata? And a lot of people will say things like metadata is data about the data, which describes exactly what we're talking about here. It describes the information, for example, that we have in databases and what it looks like. and the structure, the formats, and relevant information that relates to the data that's within databases. As you can see here, there are different levels of metadata. Object level really defines, for example, a single piece of data. and relates it as metadata. Collection level puts together a whole group of data. And then we have what's called methodological, hard word for me to say, Obviously that's derived from the word methods, but that talks about the methods that were used in order to collect, generate and process and analyze the information that ultimately we're looking at from an analysis perspective. you
